items.title: Building townhouses on a residential Canberra block could soon be easier

children.content: Townhouses, terraces and low-rise apartments will be allowed on all ACT residential blocks under a major change to planning rules proposed by the government.

items.title: David Pocock leaves door open to forming party of independents

children.content: The independent senator says Australians are frustrated with a political system that "does not work for them".

image.altText: A man in a shirt sits in a chair on a tv set talking to the host.

items.title: 'Is that a UFO?' Suspected meteor lights up night sky

children.content: Colourful streaks across the sky have been spotted across eastern Australia as a "fireball" meteor passed through the atmosphere.

image.altText: Dashcam footage showing a flashing appearing across the sky

items.title: 'Classes of 50 children': Full-day ACT public teacher strike flagged

children.content: Canberra public schools were closed until 10:30am on Friday, with more industrial action expected in June.

items.title: Fears CSIRO job cuts will damage Australia's climate change adaptability

children.content: Peak body Science and Technology Australia is concerned the CSIRO's plan to cut 92 jobs, predominantly from its environment and climate science division, will negatively affect Australia's ability to assess and adapt to climate change.

image.altText: The CSIRO logo on a sign

items.title: Million-dollar cigarette plot tied to Canberra grandmother's killer

children.content: New details have emerged about the violent 1999 home invasion that claimed Irma Palasics's life, including a separate million-dollar cigarette conspiracy that put one of her killers behind bars a decade later.

image.altText: An old photo of a man and a woman, nicely dressed, sitting next to each other smiling.

items.title: Is Canberra's CBD unsafe? It depends who you ask

children.content: Concerns about anti-social behaviour, construction and poor lighting are shaping perceptions of safety in Civic, but locals remain divided about whether Canberra's CBD feels unsafe after dark.

gallery.altText: An unknown person walks past a graffiti tunnel.

thumbnail.altText: An unknown person walks past a graffiti tunnel.

items.title: Single mothers band together to survive rising cost-of-living pressures

children.content: As rental and living costs surge, and as single mums remain one of the most vulnerable groups in the country, some are moving in together and finding many benefits.

gallery.altText: Hayly Grimmond and Anja Homburg and their children smile while siting on a bed.
Anja engages with Blair and Raelia at a kitchen bench.
Priscilla and Jenna and their children smile while sitting on a couch.

thumbnail.altText: Hayly Grimmond and Anja Homburg and their children smile while siting on a bed.
Anja engages with Blair and Raelia at a kitchen bench.
Priscilla and Jenna and their children smile while sitting on a couch.

avatar.alt: Reporter James Oaten wearing a navy button-up shirt.

items.title: Maldives cave dive deaths leave us with many theories and one big question

children.content: The question of why experienced divers would descend to depths of more than 50 metres on a single tank of air — let alone enter a cave while doing so — baffles members of the relatively close-knit deep-sea diving community.

image.altText: A deep-sea diver descends into a cave system, trailing a guide line.

items.title: Man allegedly armed with gun shot dead by police north of Brisbane

children.content: Queensland Police said the man approached officers and made threats towards them while armed with a firearm, and as a result was shot.

image.altText: Police tape in front of a narrow road with police cars behind it.

items.title: 'I've had enough': Why this loyal Labor voter is switching to One Nation

children.content: The next opportunity to test support for One Nation in Western Sydney will be the state election on March 13. Already, disillusioned Labor voter Silvana Rota is ready to "give them a go".

articles.title: Labor's tax defence is backfiring with young voters

children.content: Labor is banking on younger generations, who are overwhelmingly wage earners, being more interested in owning their own home than pocketing higher profits on a lucky crypto trade.

image.altText: Albanese is in focus as he sits on the green bench watching Chalmers speak

articles.title: 'Midwits' and media bust-ups herald One Nation's arrival in SA parliament

children.content: One Nation broke its parliamentary silence to talk about culture wars and political barneys. But not everything went as expected.

image.altText: A man wearing a suit and tie look on at a woman wearing dark top, the man is also seen in a camera viewfinder

gallery.altText: A woman wearing glasses and a purple t shirt

thumbnail.altText: A woman wearing glasses and a purple t shirt

avatar.alt: ABC reporter Nick Dole looking straight at the camera with a grey background

items.title: Labor's tax defence is backfiring with young voters

children.content: Labor is banking on younger generations, who are overwhelmingly wage earners, being more interested in owning their own home than pocketing higher profits on a lucky crypto trade.

image.altText: Albanese is in focus as he sits on the green bench watching Chalmers speak

items.title: Fifty years ago, a killer returned the knife to the kitchen and slipped away

children.content: Teenage Dawn Hillier was a "ray of sunshine". Almost 50 years later, years of investigation, a coronial inquiry, and a big reward have been unable to solve her brutal murder.

image.altText: A graphic showing murder victim Dawn Hillier

items.title: Heavyweight champ avoids shock upset with controversial stoppage

children.content: Ukraine's unbeaten heavyweight world champion Oleksandr Usyk stopped Dutch former kickboxer Rico Verhoeven with one second left in the penultimate round of their WBC title fight.

image.altText: Boxer relieved as ref stops fight, talking to defeated boxer

items.title: Export name change coming for Australian prosecco

children.content: Australia is the only country outside Italy that can use the name prosecco, but its export labels will need to change in the coming years.

image.altText: A glass of white sparkling wine held up towards the sun

items.title: Meet the women turning bicycle tyres and timber offcuts into furniture

children.content: These artists are carving out a niche in a traditionally male space, using recycled materials and offcuts to create beautiful furniture, and even making music to accompany the work.

gallery.altText: A black and white photo of a woman at a work bench constructing wooden furniture.
A low blonde wood table with a grid top and squat legs that extend beyond the table edges.

thumbnail.altText: A black and white photo of a woman at a work bench constructing wooden furniture.
A low blonde wood table with a grid top and squat legs that extend beyond the table edges.

items.title: This private island paradise has a bargain price tag, and problems galore

children.content: Makri Island, not far from mainland Greece, boasts almost 100 hectares of uninhabited Mediterranean beauty, but buyers be warned, this slice of paradise comes with a catch.

image.altText: An island, seen from above

items.title: Jamie-Lee Price thinks fake tan could solve a lot of netball's problems

children.content: Frustrated at netball's stagnation, this is what Jamie-Lee Price wants to see happen to keep her sport moving with the times.

image.altText: Netball Jamie-Lee Price sits alone in a court.

items.title: Bones found by Thai villager belonged to 27m 'Nagatitan'

children.content: It started out as a bone sticking out of the mud next to a lake in Thailand — and now scientists say it's the biggest dinosaur ever found in South-East Asia.

gallery.altText: An enormous brown bone on its end stands slightly taller than a Thai man next to it wearing all black and sneakers.
Diagram of a long-necked dinosaur skeleton in black, with a human for scale, smaller than its legs. A few bones are yellow.
Wide shot of two men in a store room with shelves behind them, one holding a bone and the other flicking through a book.

thumbnail.altText: An enormous brown bone on its end stands slightly taller than a Thai man next to it wearing all black and sneakers.
Diagram of a long-necked dinosaur skeleton in black, with a human for scale, smaller than its legs. A few bones are yellow.
Wide shot of two men in a store room with shelves behind them, one holding a bone and the other flicking through a book.

items.title: Put away the dictionary. It's spelling bee time!

children.content: Know your way around a homonym? Nah, me neither but come in and have a crack at our Sunday quiz anyway.

image.altText: Guy Montgomery beams as he stands in front of the Guy Mont set in front of a podium bearing a cup, purse and bucket.

items.title: 'There's one kid in every classroom': Teaching children to navigate grief

children.content: One in 20 Australians under the age of 18 has lost a parent, but families say grief services for children are limited. A national program teaching children how to navigate grief is trying to change that.

image.altText: A young boy being carried on the back of his mother.

items.title: Five economists share opinions on Labor's budget and Coalition's reply

children.content: Five leading economists weigh in on the budget and reply debate, embracing many of the ideas raised while also arguing that "bigger tax reform is needed."

image.altText: A man in a suit stands at a podium in parliament.

items.title: Multiple safety violations in China mine explosion, investigation finds

children.content: An explosion at a coal mine in China that killed at least 82 people was the result of the company involved having committed multiple serious safety violations, an early investigation has found.

image.altText: Several people in orange jumpsuits with bags on their backs.

items.title: 'Bushwalkers have died attempting this track': Instagram post glosses over hike's dangers

children.content: It looks beautiful in the Instagram post with blue skies and picturesque tarns carved by glaciers. But read further and the multi-day Tasmanian hike is considered one of Australia's most challenging bushwalks. In fact, it can be deadly.

gallery.altText: Procyon Peak, Western Arthur Range Dan Broun
A vertical photo of an older man holding on to the side of a cliff, with a steep drop into a valley below

thumbnail.altText: Procyon Peak, Western Arthur Range Dan Broun
A vertical photo of an older man holding on to the side of a cliff, with a steep drop into a valley below

items.title: LA suburb evacuated over fears chemical tank could explode

children.content: Evacuation orders are in place for an area covering tens of thousands of people in a suburb of Los Angeles.

image.altText: A group of people with their bags running out of a building

items.title: Trump revives centuries-old doctrine that shaped US-Cuba relations

children.content: The Monroe Doctrine was once a core tenet of US foreign policy. US President Donald Trump has brought it back with a new interpretation.

image.altText: Trump gives a thumbs up.

items.title: Mercedes duo clash as Piastri pulls off stunning overtake on Hamilton

children.content: George Russell says he did nothing wrong in a hard-fought Canadian Grand Prix sprint battle with Mercedes teammate Kimi Antonelli after the Italian questioned the Briton's defending.

gallery.altText: F1 car cuts the corner while the other takes the corner on the track
Oscar Piastri driving his F1 car on the track, coming out of a corner

thumbnail.altText: F1 car cuts the corner while the other takes the corner on the track
Oscar Piastri driving his F1 car on the track, coming out of a corner

items.title: Mystery billboard that left officials 'scratching heads' solved

children.content: Amid months of debate about the location for the 2032 Olympic rowing events, a billboard came to life. Who put it there and why was a mystery until now.

image.altText: A billboard with cartoon crocodiles rowing next to the words 'push off penrith'

items.title: Auckland makes A-League history with grand-final triumph over Sydney

children.content: Auckland has become the first side from New Zealand to win an A-League Men championship after downing competition heavyweight Sydney 1-0 in a cagey season decider.

image.altText: A soccer player in blue and black screams in celebration with floodlight behind him

items.title: NASCAR champion's cause of death confirmed by his family

children.content: NASCAR champion Kyle Busch died from severe pneumonia that turned into sepsis, according to a statement provided by his family on Saturday.

image.altText: A general view of an LED screen displaying a tribute to Kyle Busch

items.title: Socceroo injured ahead of World Cup as Premier League dream ends

children.content: Riley McGree's Premier League dream is over and the Socceroos star will hope his World Cup hopes are not also dashed after limping off with a hamstring injury in Middlesbrough's Championship playoff final defeat to Hull City at Wembley.

image.altText: Two players battle for the soccer ball which is at chest height

items.title: Meet the groundskeeper breathing life into a regional war cemetery

children.content: Tending to the living to honour the dead — that's what the dedicated groundskeeper of the Adelaide River War Cemetery says has been the pinnacle of his gardening career.

gallery.altText: A white man with thick, long rugged blonde beard, wearing brown wide-brim hait, khaki garden shirt and shorts.

thumbnail.altText: A white man with thick, long rugged blonde beard, wearing brown wide-brim hait, khaki garden shirt and shorts.

items.title: Endangered turtle found to have survived record-breaking flood

children.content: Experts are relieved to see the number of endangered Manning River turtles holding steady, a year after devastating flooding in northern New South Wales.

image.altText: A small turtle with a yellow stripe along its face and side, in a person's hand.

items.title: Data reveals V/Line punctuality on two routes dropped to lowest in almost a decade

children.content: The latest V/Line data reveals only 56 per cent of services arrived on time on two regional train routes during Victoria's first month of free public transport.

image.altText: Dozens of commuters getting on and off a V/Line train at a Melbourne platform.

items.title: Autumn rain in SA brings 'incredible' boom in birdlife

children.content: Parts of South Australia are preparing for an exceptional year as autumn rain brings an increase in birdlife.

gallery.altText: A pelican swims in a river and is reflected in the water.

thumbnail.altText: A pelican swims in a river and is reflected in the water.
